femsolver
  • I/O and post-processing
  • View page source

I/O and post-processing

VTK export, JSON model deck, beam force diagrams, time-history / mode-shape extraction.

I/O and post-processing utilities for femsolver models and results.

  • JSON model deck: save_model_json(), load_model_json().

  • VTK export: write_vtk() (rich), write_vtk_unstructured() (legacy / backward-compat).

  • Beam force diagrams: beam_force_diagram().

  • Extraction utilities: gather_node_history(), mode_shape_table(), capacity_curve().

femsolver.io.save_model_json(model, path)[source]
Parameters:

  • model (Model)

  • path (str | Path)

Return type:

None

femsolver.io.load_model_json(path)[source]
Parameters:

path (str | Path)

Return type:

Model

femsolver.io.write_vtk(model, path, *, deformation_scale=0.0, point_data=None, cell_data=None, include_reactions=False, include_mode_shapes=False)[source]

Write the model as a VTK legacy unstructured grid with optional extra point / cell data fields.

Parameters:

  • model (Model)

  • path (str or Path)

  • deformation_scale (float, default 0) – Scale applied to Node.disp when writing point coordinates.

  • point_data (dict of str -> ndarray) – Additional per-node fields. Scalar fields have shape (n_nodes,); vector fields have shape (n_nodes, 3). Vector fields with fewer than 3 components are zero-padded.

  • cell_data (dict of str -> ndarray) – Additional per-element fields. Shapes mirror point_data but indexed by element rather than node.

  • include_reactions (bool, default False) – If True, write Node.reaction as a 3-component vector reaction.

  • include_mode_shapes (bool, default False) – If True, write each column of Node.mode_disp as mode_1, mode_2, … vectors. Useful for visualizing eigenmodes or buckling shapes.

Return type:

None

femsolver.io.write_vtk_unstructured(model, path, deformation_scale=0.0)[source]

Write the model as a VTK legacy unstructured grid (deformed shape + nodal displacement vector).

Parameters:

  • model (Model)

  • path (str or Path) – Output file path. Conventionally .vtk.

  • deformation_scale (float) – If non-zero, displace the points by scale * Node.disp[:ndm] so that the visualization shows the deformed shape. Use 1.0 for true scale or a larger value for amplified visualization.

  • that (The displacement vector is written as POINT_DATA so)

  • the (Paraview's "Warp by Vector" filter (or similar) can apply)

  • file. (deformation interactively from a single un-warped)

Return type:

None

femsolver.io.beam_force_diagram(element, n_points=50)[source]

Sample internal forces along a 2-D beam-column element.

Parameters:

  • element (a 2-D beam-column element) – Must be one of the supported beam types in 2-D. The element must be bound to a model and must have been “recovered” (the analysis driver has run element.recover() or the element has up-to-date local forces).

  • n_points (int, default 50) – Number of sampling stations along the element axis (including both ends).

Returns:

  • dict with keys

  • * ``s`` ((n_points,) arc-length from node-1 in local x.)

  • * ``N`` ((n_points,) axial force (positive in tension).)

  • * ``V`` ((n_points,) local-y shear.)

  • * ``M`` ((n_points,) bending moment about local z.)

  • * ``length`` (float element chord length.)

Return type:

dict

Notes

For 2-D beam-column elements without distributed loads, N and V are constant and M varies linearly between the two end moments. With distributed loads, V varies linearly and M is parabolic. This implementation uses the equivalent-nodal-force vector to recover the linear/parabolic shape automatically.

femsolver.io.plot_beam_diagrams(element, n_points=50, *, ax=None, show_axial=True, show_shear=True, show_moment=True, title=None)[source]

matplotlib helper that draws N / V / M vs arc-length.

Returns the Figure object. Requires matplotlib (an optional dependency for the I/O layer).

Parameters:

  • n_points (int)

  • show_axial (bool)

  • show_shear (bool)

  • show_moment (bool)

  • title (str | None)

femsolver.io.gather_node_history(model, results, node_tag, dof)[source]

Return a single DOF’s time history from a transient analysis.

Parameters:

  • model (Model) – The model the analysis was run on (used to look up the node).

  • results (dict) – A TransientAnalysis.run() result dictionary. Must contain either "tracked_disp" (if the analysis tracked the same DOF) or per-step state captured externally.

  • node_tag (int)

  • dof (int) – DOF index within the node (0 = x, 1 = y, etc.).

Returns:

With keys "times", "disp", "node", "dof".

Return type:

dict

femsolver.io.mode_shape_table(eig_result)[source]

Turn an EigenAnalysis result into a structured table.

Parameters:

eig_result (dict) – Output of EigenAnalysis.run(). Expected keys: "frequencies_hz", "periods_s", "omegas_rad_s", optionally "modal_results" (from ResponseSpectrumAnalysis).

Return type:

dict with arrays for each tabulated quantity.

femsolver.io.capacity_curve(results, *, drift_key='tracked', force_key='lambdas')[source]

Extract a capacity curve (drift vs base shear or load factor) from a nonlinear-static or pushover analysis result.

Parameters:

  • results (dict) – Output dict from NonlinearStaticAnalysis.run(). Must contain a tracked displacement key and either explicit force history or a list of load factors / lambda values.

  • drift_key (str) – Keys to read for drift and force, respectively.

  • force_key (str) – Keys to read for drift and force, respectively.

Return type:

dict with "drift" and "force" arrays of equal length.

femsolver.io.save_ida_record(record, path)[source]

Save one IDARecord to path (HDF5).

Parameters:

  • record (IDARecord)

  • path (str)

Return type:

None

femsolver.io.load_ida_record(path, record_name=None)[source]

Load a single IDARecord from path.

If record_name is None and the file contains exactly one record, that record is returned. Otherwise the name must be given.

Parameters:

  • path (str)

  • record_name (str | None)

Return type:

IDARecord

femsolver.io.save_ida_summary(summary, path)[source]

Save a full IDASummary (multi-record + collapse) to HDF5.

Parameters:

  • summary (IDASummary)

  • path (str)

Return type:

None

femsolver.io.load_ida_summary(path)[source]

Load an IDASummary written by save_ida_summary().

Parameters:

path (str)

Return type:

IDASummary
